text.title: Regression Eval Before Fix Rule Failure Modes
text.summary: Three antipatterns observed when teams try to enforce eval-first. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Engineer opens fix PR with prompt tweak; no eval case referenced; CI green; merge; same bug returns 6 weeks later.
antipattern.root-cause: Required check not enforced or eval-first discipline missing.
antipattern.fix: Make eval-suite a required check in branch protection; PR template includes "eval_case_path" field; validator blocks merge when path empty or file absent.

antipattern.symptom: Eval case flakes 1-in-10 runs; team starts re-running CI until green instead of fixing flake.
antipattern.root-cause: Eval case uses non-zero temperature or relies on real network calls without stubs.
antipattern.fix: Re-author the case with temperature 0 + frozen tool stubs; reject the case at validator if it flakes ≥1× in 5 runs.

antipattern.symptom: Fix flips the new case but quietly breaks 3 older cases; CI shows red but team overrides "since the fix works".
antipattern.root-cause: Override allowed without counter-decision-record; fix-flip rule only checked the new case.
antipattern.fix: Require zero regressions on existing eval set; manual override only via counter-decision-record + second approver.
